text:
# Lean Principles (COE Reference)

Lean is a continuous improvement approach focused on maximizing customer value while minimizing waste. In a COE context, Lean is used to simplify workflows, reduce handoffs, and eliminate rework.

Common wastes include:
- Waiting: approvals or idle queues
- Defects or rework
- Over-processing
- Excess movement of information
- Work-in-progress backlog

Key Lean methods used in COE programs:
- Value Stream Mapping
- Standard Work
- Kaizen improvements
- Visual management dashboards
- Root cause analysis

Example:
If deviation closures are delayed, Lean helps identify bottlenecks, simplify approvals, and standardize documentation.

# Six Sigma Overview (COE Reference)

Six Sigma is a structured methodology used to reduce process variation and defects using data.

DMAIC process:
Define – Identify the problem and scope  
Measure – Collect baseline data  
Analyze – Identify root causes  
Improve – Implement solutions  
Control – Sustain improvements with monitoring

COE applications:
- deviation reduction
- batch record accuracy
- complaint cycle time improvement
- quality compliance metrics

# Operational Excellence Frameworks

Operational Excellence ensures organizations deliver consistent outcomes through structured improvement systems.

A COE provides:
- governance of improvement initiatives
- standard methodologies
- performance visibility
- training and enablement

Typical maturity levels:
Level 1 – Ad-hoc improvements  
Level 2 – Standard templates and reporting  
Level 3 – Portfolio governance and KPI tracking  
Level 4 – Continuous optimization with automation and AI

# KPI Tracking Best Practices

Good KPIs must be measurable, time-bound, and linked to business outcomes.

Types of KPIs:
Leading indicators – process metrics like cycle time  
Lagging indicators – results like cost savings

Best practices:
- define baseline before improvement
- assign KPI ownership
- track performance regularly
- maintain data consistency

COE dashboards help leadership monitor initiative progress and identify risks early.
